patches.rationale: CRITICAL: Wrong DOI for Livermore et al. (2020). 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 resolves to a CRISPR paper. Correct NMP-Siberia flux-lobe DOI is 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (matches WIN-059 prose). Curmudgeon review SEC-part2-sign-only-EXP337.c1.json queue_id=352.

Unverified DOI for Finlay et al. (2016). 10.1038/nature21807 not confirmed as Finlay SAA-drift paper. CHAOS-6 doi:10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (Finlay et al. 2016, Earth Planets Space) tabulates SAA westward drift rates and is the correct reference for the ~0.2deg/yr figure. Curmudgeon review SEC-part2-sign-only-EXP337.c1.json.
EXP-389 patch 1: TLDR ambiguity — body says 3.6× the half-width; TLDR said outside. Consistent phrasing.

patches.replace: The prediction is structured so that almost any physically plausible quiet-day result is claimed as confirmation. (A second, independent failure is documented in §4.2.7: the dome’s own published Sun and Moon altitudes produce a totality path roughly 1.3 km wide — not the 294‑km observed path.)

patches.find: The dome publishes κ as a constant; either it is, in which case 1999/2009 refute it, or it is not, in which case the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse number has no derivation. Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.
patches.replace: The dome publishes κ as a constant; either it is, in which case 1999/2009 refute it, or it is not, in which case the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse number has no derivation. Note further: reading the 10–13 µGal target through κ inversely — Δg × κ = ΔB — back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations (10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT; 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT). This implied band sits at or above the upper edge of the global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble (5–15 nT per Kim & Chang 2018, the same 207-measurement ensemble the dome itself invokes for WIN-068). A defender who assumes the dome takes a typical-ensemble ΔB of 10–13 nT for August 2026 finds that 10/1.677 ≈ 6.0 µGal and 13/1.677 ≈ 7.8 µGal — both below the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target. The arithmetic is internally consistent only if the dome commits to a ΔB premise above the typical ensemble; that premise itself is unpublished in any dome material we have located. Either the dome publishes its quantitative August 2026 ΔB prediction in advance (in which case the κ derivation becomes auditable in advance — a symmetric strengthening of the vise) or it does not (in which case the 10–13 µGal target’s derivation has an additional unspecified premise beyond κ). Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.
patches.rationale: EXP-384-WIN-014 patch 1: Make implied August 2026 ΔB premise explicit. 10-13 µGal through κ=1.677 nT/µGal back-implies 16.8-21.8 nT — at or above typical totality ensemble upper bound. ISS-1957 major severity orphan.

patches.find: κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target — addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection.
patches.replace: κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target (which, through κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations — at or above the upper edge of the global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble of 5–15 nT cited for WIN-068) — addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection.
patches.rationale: EXP-384-WIN-014 patch 2 (WIN-014): Add ΔB back-calculation cross-ref. ISS-1957 major severity orphan.
